candidates.name: Skill orchestration pipeline (10-step, parallel agents)
candidates.description: 10-step pipeline with parallel agent dispatch, cache checking, chunk sizing. Most structured multi-step skill example found. Directly comparable to SoNash skill architecture.

candidates.name: Confidence tagging taxonomy (EXTRACTED/INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS)
candidates.description: Three-tier confidence taxonomy for graph edges. EXTRACTED = directly observed, INFERRED = model-reasoned, AMBIGUOUS = uncertain. Applicable to analysis findings and recall results.

candidates.name: MCP server pattern (stateless, token-budgeted)
candidates.description: 320-line MCP stdio server. Load graph once, expose 7 tools. Token budget on subgraph rendering. No network listener.

candidates.name: LanguageConfig dataclass (generic walker + per-type config)
candidates.description: One config object per language with type-specific node resolution, call detection, import handling. Generic walker uses config to extract 20 languages with zero duplication.

candidates.name: Threat-vector security model format
candidates.description: Security model organized by attack vector with mitigations table and 'does NOT do' section. More readable than checklist-based formats.

candidates.name: Platform-specific skill variants
candidates.description: 7 platform-specific skill files with shared core and platform-specific adaptations (Agent vs Task tool, hook availability, sequential fallback).

candidates.name: ARCHITECTURE.md single-page format
candidates.description: Pipeline, module table, extraction schema, confidence labels, how-to-extend, security, testing — all on one page. No bloat.

candidates.name: Graph-only persistence without versioning
candidates.description: graph.json has no schema_version, no migration path. Format changes break old graphs silently. Our analysis.json schema_version is the right approach.

candidates.name: Oversized skill files (15K+ tokens per variant)
candidates.description: 7 skill files with significant content duplication. 15K+ tokens each. LLM context waste and maintenance burden.

candidates.name: Ship fast skip review pattern
candidates.description: 20 releases in 6 days with zero merged external PRs. Security mitigations are self-assessed only. Impressive velocity but fragile.

cross_repo_connections.detail: Both use SKILL.md-based skill architecture for Claude Code. CLI-Anything has 37 skill files. graphify has 7 platform variants.
MemSkill is a memory skill for Claude Code. graphify's graph persistence is an alternative to MemSkill's memory approach.
